title: PilotFish Control Plane Outage - Config Service Failure
root_cause: Config-Service ran out of disk space due to unrotated audit logs, causing PilotFish-Core to fail its startup health check loop and enter a crash loop.

timeline.event: Monitoring alerts fire for PilotFish-Core health check failures
Root cause identified: disk full on Config-Service node cfg-node-02
Emergency disk space freed by archiving old audit logs
PilotFish-API and Scheduler restored, incident closed

resolution_steps: Identified disk full condition on Config-Service node cfg-node-02
Archived audit logs older than 30 days to cold storage: tar -czf /archive/audit-$(date +%Y%m%d).tar.gz /var/log/config-service/audit/
Freed 42GB of disk space, bringing utilization from 100% to 58%
Restarted PilotFish-Core in normal mode (no data corruption detected)
Validated end-to-end by submitting test workflow
lessons_learned: Add disk space monitoring alert at 80% threshold for Config-Service nodes
Implement automatic log rotation for Config-Service audit logs (daily, retain 7 days)
Add disk space pre-flight check to PilotFish-Core startup sequence
Update TSG to include disk space check in pre-recovery checklist
contributing_factors: Log rotation policy not configured for Config-Service audit logs
Disk monitoring threshold was set too high (95%) to provide adequate warning time
No automated remediation for disk space issues
